replication_study.hypothesis: Among early childhood educators, weekend relaxation (mean of REQ relaxation items measured on Sunday evening) positively predicts joviality at the end of the following workweek (PANAS-X joviality scale measured the next Friday), controlling for baseline joviality (previous Friday), other weekend recovery experiences (detachment, mastery, control), weekend hassles, and demographics (age, gender, children, work hours, work days).

data_plan.dataset_identifier: k17 weekend recovery survey (processed: k17_processed_data.csv, created from Raw data/k17_t1_data.csv, k17_t2_data.csv, k17_t3_data.csv)
data_plan.source_type: Longitudinal online survey (Qualtrics), three time points: pre-weekend (Friday), post-weekend (Sunday), and end of following workweek (Friday)
data_plan.wave_or_subset: Participants who completed all three time points and passed finishing checks; duplicates by email removed keeping the first instance; timing windows computed via Wave variable (k17_data_prep.R).
data_plan.sample_size: 339 participants with complete T1, T2, T3 in processed file (data/original/26/input/replication_data/k17_processed_data.csv; get_dataset_shape shows 339 rows).
data_plan.access_details: Data provided locally in /app/data for replication. No additional access restrictions for this task.

qualification.explanation: This dataset is well-suited for a close replication: it uses the same constructs (REQ, PANAS-X) measured at the same three time points and includes the same control structure. It is a different sample collected under similar conditions, thus testing the robustness of the original claim.
qualification.similarity_to_original: Both studies implement a longitudinal survey of preschool/early childhood educators, measuring recovery experiences (detachment, mastery, control, relaxation) and discrete affect via PANAS-X at three time points. The replication’s variables map directly (e.g., req_relax_1..4, T1_panas_jov_1..8, T3_panas_jov_1..8, hassles) as documented in Data_Code_Book.csv and k17_data_dictionary.csv (replication_data folder). The original paper and post_registration.json describe the same design with Time 1 (Friday), Time 2 (Sunday), Time 3 (following Friday), using the REQ and PANAS-X instruments and including the same demographic/work controls. Sources: data/original/26/input/post_registration.json (original sample N=229, instruments and timing) and data/original/26/input/original_paper.pdf (PDF summary); replication variable mapping in data/original/26/input/replication_data/Data_Code_Book.csv.
qualification.deviation_from_original: The replication is a new sample (processed N=339 vs. original N=229 per post_registration.json); collected in 2021 (see German weekday stamps in raw data heads and Data_Code_Book.csv; Raw data files show dates in 2021). While the original recruited preschool teachers in Germany, this dataset references early childhood educators (Data_Code_Book.csv: work_years description). The replication also records a broad hassles inventory and uplifts list (has_1..53, upl_1..53) but uses hassles consistent with the original focal test. The processed dataset deduplicates by email and selects participants who finished all three time points (k17_data_prep.R), which may differ from original sample curation. Citations: post_registration.json (original timing, sample size), replication_data/Data_Code_Book.csv (variables, roles), replication_data raw CSV heads showing dated stamps and Finished flags.

data_plan.notes: Key derived variables are computed using rowMeans/rowSums with na.rm=TRUE. Age is 2021 - birthyear; children recoded to binary. Gender coded 1=male, 2=female (Data_Code_Book.csv). Processed dataset was deduplicated by RecipientEmail, potentially reducing multiple submissions to first occurrence. lm() will perform listwise deletion if any predictors/outcomes are missing. Pre-registration data-preparation script (k17_data_preparation_preregistration.R) references T1/T2/T3 without reading them; use k17_data_prep.R instead. All read/write paths have been updated to /app/data in k17_data_prep.R and k17_analysis.R to comply with runtime constraints.

steps: Ensure the raw CSV files are present under /app/data/Raw data/: k17_t1_data.csv, k17_t2_data.csv, k17_t3_data.csv.

Run k17_data_prep.R to: (a) filter participants present in all waves and Finished == 1; (b) compute Wave and deduplicate emails; (c) rename columns; (d) compute REQ (relax, mastery, control, detachment), hassles, and PANAS-X scales (T1 baseline and T3 outcomes); (e) recode demographics; (f) save /app/data/k17_processed_data.csv.
Run k17_analysis.R to load /app/data/k17_processed_data.csv and fit hierarchical linear regressions: Model_1 (demographics), Model_2 (+ T1 joviality), Model_3 (+ weekend REQ and hassles).
Extract the focal coefficient: effect of req_relax on T3_panas_joviality in Model_3; record estimate, standard error, t-value, one-tailed p-value, and sign.
Optionally, compare standardized (Model_3) and unstandardized (Model_3a) estimates.
planned_method.models: Hierarchical multiple linear regression: T3_panas_joviality ~ controls (age, gender, children, work_hours, work_days) + baseline T1_panas_joviality + weekend REQ (req_relax, req_mastery, req_control, req_detach) + hassles. Primary inference on the coefficient for req_relax.
planned_method.outcome_variable: T3_panas_joviality (mean of T3 joviality items 1–8 as computed in k17_data_prep.R).
planned_method.independent_variables: req_relax (mean of REQ relaxation items 1–4 measured at Time 2).
planned_method.control_variables: Age (derived from birthyear), gender (1=male, 2=female), children (0/1), work_hours per week, work_days per week; baseline T1_panas_joviality; other weekend recovery experiences (req_detach, req_mastery, req_control); weekend hassles (sum of has_1..53).
planned_method.tools_software: R (base stats), optional dplyr for data handling in prereg script; Rscript execution. Python script provided but not required for the primary replication pipeline.

planned_estimation_and_test.estimation: The (standardized and unstandardized) regression coefficient for req_relax in Model_3 predicting T3_panas_joviality.
planned_estimation_and_test.test: One-tailed t-test of H0: beta_relax <= 0 vs. H1: beta_relax > 0 at alpha=0.05. Also report two-tailed p-value for transparency.

planned_method.missing_data_handling: Participants must have Finished==1 at T1, T2, T3. Scale scores computed with na.rm=TRUE. lm() will use listwise deletion for rows with missing model variables.
planned_method.multiple_testing_policy: Single focal test; no correction. Exploratory models (other PANAS outcomes) are secondary with no formal adjustment; interpret cautiously.
planned_method.inference_criteria: Replication is considered successful if the req_relax coefficient in Model_3 is positive and statistically significant at alpha=0.05 (one-tailed), consistent with the original β≈0.15 (post_registration.json and original_paper.pdf summary). Secondary checks: similar magnitude to original and positive R² change from Model_2 to Model_3.

files.k17_data_prep.R: Main data preparation script. Reads raw Qualtrics CSVs from /app/data/Raw data, harmonizes columns and labels, filters complete cases, computes REQ scales, hassles, and PANAS T1/T3 scales, derives demographics (age, children), deduplicates, and writes /app/data/k17_processed_data.csv. Paths updated to /app/data.
files.k17_analysis.R: Primary analysis script. Loads /app/data/k17_processed_data.csv (if score_all is not in memory) and runs hierarchical linear models including the focal Model_3 on standardized variables plus an uncentered Model_3a. Paths updated to /app/data.
files.k17_data_preparation_preregistration.R: Alternate/prereg data preparation draft. Assumes T1/T2/T3 are already loaded; computes indices and merges by RecipientEmail. Not used in the main pipeline; references to /app/data not required for replication after opting for k17_data_prep.R.
files.k17_analysis_preregistration.R: Alternate/prereg analysis script on unstandardized variables. Modified to load /app/data/k17_processed_data.csv if not in memory. Provides similar hierarchical models for comparison.
files.k17_data_prep__py.py: Prototype Python data-prep. Reads raw CSVs from /app/data and writes /app/data/k17_processed_data.csv. Not used in the primary pipeline and may not fully align with all variable constructions (e.g., Wave handling, full PANAS mapping).
files.Data_Code_Book.csv: Detailed variable names, allowed values, and descriptions for processed variables and scales.
files.k17_data_dictionary.csv: Additional variable dictionary aligning survey items to scale constructs.
files.Raw data/k17_t2_data.csv: Raw Time 2 (Sunday after weekend) responses, includes REQ and hassles/uplifts.

codebase.notes: Entry order: run k17_data_prep.R first, then k17_analysis.R. Paths have been corrected to /app/data in k17_data_prep.R (reads and writes) and k17_analysis.R (reads). The prereg preparation script is not standalone (expects T1/T2/T3 already in memory) and is not required for replication execution. Dependencies are minimal (base R); dplyr is used in prereg script only.

instructions: Place the raw CSVs under ./data/Raw data/: k17_t1_data.csv, k17_t2_data.csv, k17_t3_data.csv.

In the container, run: Rscript /app/data/../path/to/k17_data_prep.R (ensure working directory can find the script file; alternatively run from repo root and source the script). This produces /app/data/k17_processed_data.csv.
Run: Rscript /app/data/../path/to/k17_analysis.R. This loads /app/data/k17_processed_data.csv and fits Model_1, Model_2, and the focal Model_3.
Record the coefficient for req_relax in Model_3 (estimate, SE, t, p) and compare against the original β≈0.15, p<0.01 (post_registration.json and original_paper.pdf summary).
comparison_metrics: Sign and one-tailed p-value for the req_relax coefficient in Model_3 (target: positive and p<0.05).
Magnitude of standardized beta compared to original (target around 0.15).
Incremental R² from Model_2 to Model_3 (should be positive).
Confidence interval overlap with positive region (optional).
